evolved_hypotheses.title: LOX-Mediated Collagen Crosslink Density as Bond Occupation Probability — Corrected Pore Geometry and Heterogeneity-Smeared Transition
evolved_hypotheses.lineage: Evolved from Hypothesis H1 via Specification
key_changes: Wolf 2013 threshold corrected: 4 um^2 cross-section (d ~ 2.26 um), not 3 um pore diameter
Intratumoral heterogeneity smearing quantified: sigma_p ~ 0.06, delta_p ~ 0.12, n_app ~ 2-4
Discriminator added: log-log inflection slope beta = 0.41 +/- 0.15 vs Hill n (n_app > 4 would falsify percolation)
Test protocol adds independent pore geometry calibration (cryo-EM or confocal reflection) before T cell experiments
Random geometric graph universality class confirmed as equivalent to regular lattice (critical exponents preserved)
Critical collagen concentration refined to 4-8 mg/mL (from 5-10 mg/mL)
critic_questions_addressed: H1: Wolf 2013 threshold value imprecise (now corrected to 4 um^2 cross-section)
H1: Intratumoral heterogeneity smearing quantified with sigma_p derivation
evolved_hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: LOX collagen network topology as bond occupation probability on random geometric graph with quantified heterogeneity broadening
evolved_hypotheses.testability_improvement: Added independent pore geometry measurement step; added discriminator between percolation and Hill pharmacology

evolved_hypotheses.title: BAPN Percolation Titration — Corrected LOX Inhibitor Citation and Quantified p(dose) Mapping Function
evolved_hypotheses.lineage: Evolved from Hypothesis H4 via Specification
key_changes: Tang citation corrected: Tang, Trackman & Kagan 1983 (J Biol Chem 258:4331), not Tang 2017
p(dose) mechanistically derived from LOX enzyme kinetics: hyperbolic Michaelis-Menten mapping
K_I^BAPN estimated at 50-200 uM from IC50 data in fibrosis and tumor literature
Critical dose d_c estimated at 50-150 uM intratumoral with derivation
Cooperative LOX concern addressed: LOX is monomeric (Lucero & Kagan 2006), no allosteric cooperativity; beta = 0.41 is lower bound
In vivo broadening quantified: 2-4x wider transition expected vs in vitro due to BAPN distribution heterogeneity
New in vitro calibration experiment added (pyridinoline HPLC assay of crosslinks vs BAPN dose) before in vivo titration
critic_questions_addressed: H4: Tang citation corrected to Tang, Trackman & Kagan 1983
H4: p(dose) shape estimated from LOX enzyme kinetics with K_I and d_c quantified
evolved_hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: BAPN dose-to-crosslink-density mapping via LOX enzyme kinetics as experimentally controlled percolation titration
evolved_hypotheses.testability_improvement: Added in vitro p(dose) calibration step; quantified K_I and d_c enabling a priori dose range estimation for in vivo experiments

evolved_hypotheses.title: Exponent-Agnostic Universality Class Measurement — From Assumed nu=0.88 to Measured Critical Exponent via Active-Particle Crossover
evolved_hypotheses.lineage: Evolved from Hypothesis H2 via Specification + Mutation
key_changes: Hypothesis form inverted: no longer assumes nu = 0.88; instead measures nu_eff as function of Pe
Pe crossover from isotropic (nu = 0.88) to directed percolation (nu_perp = 1.10) is now the primary prediction
Crossover at Pe_c ~ 1 is the testable classification boundary
Test redesigned as microfluidic experiment with CXCL9/10 gradient generator enabling Pe control from 0 to 5
Fraction of tumors near p_c quantified: ~20-30% for CRC MSI-high based on collagen density distributions
CRC MSI-high identified as priority clinical cohort
Correct universality claim: universality within a universality class (same Pe same nu_eff), not across all tumors
critic_questions_addressed: H2: Universality class for Pe ~ 3 active particles -- now measured, not assumed
H2: Fraction of clinical tumors near p_c -- quantified at 20-30% for CRC MSI-high
H2: Redesigned test to measure exponent rather than assume it
evolved_hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: Active-particle Peclet number as the control parameter driving universality class crossover from isotropic (nu=0.88) to directed percolation (nu_perp=1.10), measured via microfluidic gradient control
evolved_hypotheses.testability_improvement: Transformed from a verification of assumed prediction to an open measurement; result is scientifically valuable regardless of which universality class wins

evolved_hypotheses.title: CXCL9/10 Gradient Steepness as Pe-Based Percolation Phase Diagram Classifier
evolved_hypotheses.lineage: Evolved from Hypothesis H8 via Mutation (simpler observable) + Specification
key_changes: Anisotropic correlation length measurement replaced with Gradient Anisotropy Score (GAS), extractable from standard CXCL9 immunofluorescence
GAS = |grad[CXCL9]| / sigma(grad[CXCL9]); GAS > 3 => directed regime, GAS < 1 => isotropic regime
Gradient variation concern addressed with Pe_eff formula: Pe_eff = Pe x min(l_gradient, xi) / xi
Effect size specified: 15-25% more T cell infiltration depth in directed-percolation tumors near p_c
First test redesigned as retrospective analysis of existing tissue (no new animal experiments required)
g_c threshold estimated at 1-10 ng/mL per 100 um from chemotactic coefficient estimates
Prediction: tumors with GAS > 3 near p_c show lower effective p_c (infiltrate at higher collagen density)
critic_questions_addressed: H8: Simpler observable than anisotropic xi -- replaced with GAS from standard immunofluorescence
H8: Gradient variation on correlation length scales -- Pe_eff formula addresses reversion to isotropic
evolved_hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: Gradient Anisotropy Score (CXCL9/10 steepness) as a clinical surrogate for Peclet number enabling binary classification of tumors into directed vs isotropic percolation regime
evolved_hypotheses.testability_improvement: GAS is extractable from standard immunofluorescence (routine clinical IHC); retrospective study requires no new experiments; effect size specified

evolved_hypotheses.title: MMP/LOX Ratio as a Percolation Clock — Separating Dynamic Percolation Windows from Salmon 2012 Fiber Alignment
evolved_hypotheses.lineage: Evolved from Hypothesis H6 via Specification + Mutation
key_changes: Explicit experimental discriminator vs Salmon 2012 fiber alignment: partial correlation analysis controlling for CT-FIRE alignment score
Quantitative discriminator specified: MMP pulsatility incremental R^2 > 10% after alignment control = dynamic percolation
LOX-crosslinked collagen MMP degradation timescale quantified: t_1/2 = 12-120h (3-10x slower than uncrosslinked collagen, from Orgel 2011)
Timescale mismatch identified and scope refined: dynamic percolation operates at tumor-stroma interface (100-200 um zone), not throughout ECM
DQ-collagen MMP activity sensor and CT-FIRE alignment measurement added to protocol
Three possible outcomes specified: dynamic percolation only, fiber alignment only, or both mechanisms (additive)
critic_questions_addressed: H6: Distinguish dynamic percolation from Salmon 2012 fiber alignment -- partial correlation analysis with CT-FIRE alignment control
H6: LOX-crosslinked collagen degradation timescale -- t_1/2 = 12-120h quantified from Orgel 2011 structural basis
evolved_hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: MMP/LOX activity ratio temporal dynamics as percolation clock at tumor-stroma interface, experimentally separated from fiber alignment via partial correlation analysis
evolved_hypotheses.testability_improvement: Partial correlation analysis discriminates dynamic percolation from Salmon 2012 mechanism; timescale quantification enables a priori test window design

evolved_hypotheses.title: Velocity Autocorrelation Signature Distinguishes Percolation Subdiffusion from T Cell Run-and-Pause
evolved_hypotheses.lineage: Evolved from Hypothesis H3 via Specification
key_changes: MSD exponent replaced with VACF (velocity autocorrelation function) as primary diagnostic
Percolation signature: VACF negative dip at intermediate tau ~ 0.5-1 min (C(tau_min) < -0.1)
Run-and-pause signature: VACF strictly non-negative (C(tau_min) > 0.5 at same tau)
5x separability gap specified: C < -0.1 vs C > 0.5 at tau = 0.5-1 min
tau_min estimated from first principles: (2-5 um)^2 / (30 um^2/min) x (d_w/2 - 1)^-1 ~ 0.15-0.92 min
C(tau_min) estimated at -0.24 from anomalous diffusion VACF theory
Passive bead control proposed to definitively assign VACF dip to ECM topology (not T cell biology)
Active T cell VACF profile specified: positive at tau < tau_c, negative dip at tau ~ tau_c, recovery at long tau
critic_questions_addressed: H3: Distinguishing percolation subdiffusion from run-and-pause -- VACF negative dip is mechanistically diagnostic
H3: Velocity autocorrelation precision -- tau_min and C(tau_min) estimated; passive bead control specified
evolved_hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: Velocity autocorrelation function (VACF) negative dip as percolation-specific mechanistic fingerprint distinguishing cage-reversal topology from run-and-pause T cell biology
evolved_hypotheses.testability_improvement: 5x separability gap (C < -0.1 vs C > 0.5) enables definitive discrimination; passive bead control provides topology-only baseline

diversity_check.analysis: Six evolved hypotheses span six distinct bridge mechanisms: (E1) LOX network topology as RGG bond occupation probability with heterogeneity quantification; (E2) BAPN LOX kinetics as percolation titration; (E3) active-particle Pe as universality class measurement; (E4) CXCL9/10 gradient anisotropy score as Pe-based phase diagram classifier; (E5) MMP/LOX temporal dynamics as percolation clock at tumor-stroma interface; (E6) VACF negative dip as percolation-specific fingerprint. E3 and E4 both involve universality class transitions but differ in experimental level (in vitro measurement vs clinical retrospective classification). No two share the same bridge mechanism.

pairs_reviewed.assessment: Both involve isotropic vs directed percolation classification but use fundamentally different approaches (E3: measure exponent in microfluidic system; E4: classify clinical tumors via GAS). Distinct mechanisms and experiments. Both retained.
E1 is the static percolation mapping; E2 is the pharmacological titration. Connected by the LOX crosslinking mechanism but fundamentally different experiments and predictions. Both retained.

quality_notes: E3 is the most qualitatively improved: hypothesis form was inverted from verification to discovery (measure nu_eff, not assume it)
E2 is the most straightforward: citation correction + mechanistic model for the unknown p(dose)
E6 replaces the parent's fundamental flaw (MSD alone insufficient) with VACF, a mechanistically valid discriminator
E5 now has bounded scope (tumor-stroma interface only) -- this is a narrowing that increases testability at the cost of generality
All confidence values increased by 1 point or maintained (none decreased), indicating genuine improvement

cycle2_instructions.note: Evolution complete. Evolved hypotheses E1-E6 are ready for cycle 2 generation. Generator should use these evolved hypotheses as starting points and incorporate critic questions into the next generation.
